stem: A circle with center O has a circumference of 48. Two perpendicular diameters of the circle meet at O. The endpoints of one diameter on the circle are labeled P and R, and the endpoints of the other diameter on the circle are labeled Q and S. What is the length of minor arc PQ?
rationale:
Choice B is correct. A circle has 360° of arc. Because the two diameters are perpendicular, central angle POQ has measure 90°. Therefore minor arc PQ is 90/360 = 1/4 of the circumference. Since the circumference is 48, the length of minor arc PQ is (1/4) × 48 = 12.

Choice A uses 1/8 of the circumference. Choice C uses 1/2 of the circumference (a semicircle). Choice D is the full circumference, not the arc.
